project.title: Connecting exoproteome content and Monilinia laxa virulence
project.projectDescription: LC-MS/MS proteomic studies using in vitro peach cultures provided data on exoproteomes of the three isolates at equivalent stages of brown rot colonization
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Erlenmeyer flask containing 1% freeze-dried peach were inoculated with the conidial suspension of each isolate to a final concentration of 10^5 conidia ml-1. Proteins present in the culture media were precipitated by adding trichloroacetic acid to a final concentration of 10%. Samples were incubated on ice for 20 min, centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 10 min at 4ºC to collect precipitated proteins. The pellets were washed firstly with 1 ml of ethanol-ethyl ether (1:1), centrifuged at 13,000 rpm for 10 min at 4ºC and then secondly with 1 ml of ethanol-ethyl ether (1:3) followed by centrifugation at 13,000 rpm for 10 min at 4ºC. Protein pellets from exoproteome were dried at room temperature and then dissolved in Laemmli cracking buffer and digested with trypsin for LC-MS/MS
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Exoproteome of each isolate sample raw files were searched with Sequest through Proteome Discoverer version 1.4.1.14 against M. laxa 8L proteome (Naranjo-Ortíz et al., 2018) with peptide tolerance of 10 ppm and fragment tolerance of 0.5 Da. Cysteine carbamidomethylation and methionine oxidation were considered fixed modifications. False discovery rate calculations were generated using Percolator at q ≤ 0,01 (Käll et al., 2007).

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Comment[Instrument]: nano Easy nLC 1000 (Proxeon) coupled to an LTQ–Orbitrap Velos (Thermo Scientific)
Comment[Separation]: Peptides were loaded onto Acclaim PepMap 100 (Thermo Scientific) trap column and eluted onto Acclaim PepMap 100 C18 3 μm (Thermo Scientific, 75 μm x 25 cm).
Comment[GradientTime]: A 110-min gradient was run at 250 nl/min flow rate using gradients from 0% to 35% buffer B (0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile) 90 min, from 45% to 95% buffer B 10 min, 95% buffer B 9 min and 10% buffer B 1 min.
Comment[FractionationMethod]: A 20-μl aliquot of extracellular protein precipitate was loaded onto a 10% polyacrylamide gel and allowed to run 1 cm into the resolving gel. The piece of the gel was excised and proteins were treated with trypsin.
